agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML/sanctions screening), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.","bounded_context":"Wallet Transfers & Account Security — covers identity verification tiers, transfer execution, sanctions screening, account locking, and transaction notifications. Adjacent contexts (not defined here) likely include Ledger/Settlement, Customer Support, and Identity Provider.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process that determines a user's verification tier and what wallet operations they are permitted to perform."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has completed full KYC. May send, receive, and withdraw funds. Daily transfer limit: $5,000."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account that has completed some but not all KYC steps. May send and receive funds with a daily transfer limit of $500. Cannot withdraw (not specified — see escalations)."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account with no KYC completion. May only receive money. Cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Settlement","definition":"The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation). Once settled, a transaction is irreversible."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"A person, organization, or country appearing on a regulatory sanctions list (e.g., OFAC SDN). Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked before execution."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. A locked account cannot be accessed until a support agent manually unlocks it."},{"term":"Daily Transfer Limit","definition":"The maximum cumulative outbound transfer amount a user may initiate within a rolling or calendar day, determined by verification tier."},{"term":"Transaction Notification","definition":"An alert sent to the user for any transaction exceeding $1,000 in value."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Users must complete full KYC before they can send money.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory requirement. Allowing an unverified user to send money is a compliance violation that could result in fines or license revocation. This is not a policy toggle — it is a legal gate.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to initiate an outbound transfer from an account with verification_status = UNVERIFIED must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Directly tied to BR-1. Receiving funds into an unverified account is permitted (funds are held), but no outbound movement is allowed. Violating this corrupts the compliance posture of the platform.","testable_assertion":"An inbound transfer to an UNVERIFIED account succeeds. An outbound transfer or withdrawal request from an UNVERIFIED account is rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Daily transfer limit for verified accounts is $5,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy threshold. A VP or compliance officer could raise or lower this limit based on risk appetite, market conditions, or regulatory guidance. The system must enforce *a* limit — the specific number is policy.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers for a VERIFIED account within the limit window must not exceed $5,000. A transfer that would breach this limit is rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Daily transfer limit for partially-verified accounts is $500.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Same reasoning as BR-3 — a tunable policy threshold, not a physical or data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers for a PARTIALLY_VERIFIED account within the limit window must not exceed $500. A transfer that would breach this limit is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A transaction cannot be reversed once it has been settled (T+1).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Settlement finality is a core financial system invariant. If a settled transaction could be reversed through normal flows, the ledger loses integrity and reconciliation breaks. Reversals post-settlement require a *new* compensating transaction, not a reversal of the original.","testable_assertion":"Any reversal request for a transaction with status = SETTLED must be rejected. Only transactions with status = PENDING (pre-settlement) may be reversed."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory/legal mandate (OFAC, EU sanctions). Allowing a sanctioned transfer to execute — even once — is a reportable compliance violation. This is not discretionary.","testable_assertion":"Before any outbound transfer is executed, the recipient must be screened against the active sanctions list. If a match is found, the transfer is blocked and the event is logged for compliance review."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Five consecutive failed login attempts lock the account. Only a support agent can unlock it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Account security invariant. If a locked account can be accessed without support intervention, the security model is compromised. The lock threshold (5) is policy-tunable, but the lock mechanism itself and the requirement for human unlock are integrity constraints.","testable_assertion":"After 5 consecutive failed login attempts, account.status = LOCKED. A LOCKED account rejects all login attempts. Only an action by an authenticated support agent can transition account.status from LOCKED to ACTIVE. The failed attempt counter resets on successful login."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A notification is sent for any transaction over $1,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a user-experience and transparency policy. The $1,000 threshold is a business decision that could be changed, and notification preferences could evolve. Failure to notify does not corrupt system state — it degrades user experience and may have regulatory implications, but the system remains consistent.","testable_assertion":"When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is created (inbound or outbound), a notification event is emitted to the account holder. Transactions at exactly $1,000 do not trigger the notification."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user with an UNVERIFIED account","when":"They attempt to send money to another user","then":"The transfer is rejected with an error indicating KYC completion is required"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user with an UNVERIFIED account","when":"Another user sends them $200","then":"The transfer succeeds and the funds are credited to the unverified account's balance"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A user with a VERIFIED account who has already sent $4,800 today","when":"They attempt to send $300","then":"The transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A user with a PARTIALLY_VERIFIED account who has sent $0 today","when":"They attempt to send $500","then":"The transfer succeeds as it is within the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A user with a PARTIALLY_VERIFIED account who has sent $400 today","when":"They attempt to send $200","then":"The transfer is rejected because cumulative $600 exceeds the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A transaction was initiated yesterday and has reached SETTLED status (T+1)","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is rejected with a message indicating settled transactions cannot be reversed"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A transaction is in PENDING status (initiated today, not yet settled)","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is processed and funds are returned to the sender"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A verified user attempts to send $2,000 to a recipient","when":"The recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list","then":"The transfer is blocked before execution, the user is informed the transfer cannot be completed, and the event is logged for compliance review"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A user has entered their password incorrectly 4 times consecutively","when":"They fail a 5th login attempt","then":"The account status transitions to LOCKED and all subsequent login attempts are rejected regardless of correctness"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A user account is in LOCKED status","when":"The user enters the correct password","then":"The login is still rejected because only a support agent can unlock the account"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A user account is in LOCKED status","when":"An authenticated support agent unlocks the account","then":"The account status transitions to ACTIVE and the user can log in with correct credentials"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A verified user sends $1,500 to another user","when":"The transaction is created","then":"A notification is sent to the account holder about the transaction"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A verified user sends $1,000 to another user","when":"The transaction is created","then":"No notification is triggered (threshold is strictly over $1,000)"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A user has failed login 3 times, then succeeds on the 4th attempt","when":"They subsequently fail 2 more times","then":"The account is NOT locked because the consecutive failure counter reset on the successful login"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Transfer amount exactly equals remaining daily limit","expected_behavior":"Transfer succeeds. Limit enforcement is <= $5,000 / $500 cumulative, so hitting the boundary exactly is permitted."},{"scenario":"Recipient becomes sanctioned between transaction initiation and settlement","expected_behavior":"Spec does not address this. Recommended: sanctions screening should occur at execution time AND at settlement time. Escalated as ambiguity."},{"scenario":"User is mid-KYC (partially verified) — can they withdraw?","expected_behavior":"Spec says unverified cannot withdraw but does not explicitly state whether partially-verified can withdraw. Escalated as ambiguity."},{"scenario":"Notification threshold for incoming transactions","expected_behavior":"Spec says 'any transaction over $1,000' — this should apply to both inbound and outbound. Receiving $5,000 should also trigger a notification to the recipient."},{"scenario":"Account locked during an in-flight transaction","expected_behavior":"Locking applies to login/authentication. An already-authenticated session with an in-flight transaction should likely complete, but spec does not clarify. The lock should not corrupt in-progress financial operations."},{"scenario":"Daily limit window definition — calendar day vs rolling 24h","expected_behavior":"Spec says 'daily' but does not define the window. Calendar-day (midnight reset in user's timezone) vs rolling 24-hour window produces different outcomes. Escalated as ambiguity."},{"scenario":"Multiple sanctioned entity lists with conflicting data","expected_behavior":"Spec says 'sanctioned entities' generically. In practice, the platform must define which lists are authoritative (OFAC SDN, EU consolidated list, UN, etc.). Escalated as ambiguity."},{"scenario":"Notification delivery failure","expected_behavior":"Spec requires notification but doesn't define what happens if delivery fails. The transaction should NOT be rolled back due to notification failure — notification is a side effect, not a gate."},{"scenario":"Self-transfer (send to own account)","expected_behavior":"Spec doesn't exclude self-transfers. They should still count toward daily limits and still require KYC, but may not need sanctions screening against self."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Non-compliance with KYC/sanctions rules exposes the platform to regulatory fines (potentially millions), account freezes by banking partners, and loss of money transmitter license. Daily limits manage exposure to fraud losses. Settlement finality prevents double-spend scenarios that would create ledger imbalances.","operational":"Account locking on failed logins will generate support ticket volume — support team must be staffed for unlock requests. Sanctions screening adds latency to every outbound transfer; the screening service becomes a critical dependency. Daily limit tracking requires reliable, low-latency aggregation of today's transfers per account.","user":"Unverified users have a severely restricted experience (receive-only), creating friction that must be offset by a smooth KYC onboarding flow. Account locking after 5 attempts will frustrate legitimate users who forget passwords — the UX must clearly communicate remaining attempts. The $1,000 notification threshold provides transparency but may be noisy for high-volume users.","risk":"Highest risk: sanctions screening failure (regulatory/legal). Second: KYC bypass (compliance). Third: settlement reversal bug (financial integrity). Account locking is a moderate risk — too aggressive locks out legitimate users; too lenient enables brute-force attacks. The 5-attempt threshold is a reasonable balance but should be monitored.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Wallet Holders)","impact":"Tiered access based on KYC status affects usability. Account locking affects access. Notifications provide transparency on large transactions."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Owns sanctions list updates, KYC policy, and regulatory reporting. Must be notified of all blocked sanctions transfers. Settlement finality rules must align with regulatory requirements."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Sole authority to unlock accounts. Will see ticket volume proportional to login failure rates. Must have secure, auditable unlock tooling."},{"group":"Finance / Treasury","impact":"Settlement finality (T+1) affects cash flow and reconciliation. Daily limits bound exposure per account."},{"group":"Engineering / Platform","impact":"Must build reliable sanctions screening, real-time daily limit tracking, and idempotent settlement processing. Sanctions screening is on the critical path for every transfer."},{"group":"Banking Partners / Payment Processors","impact":"Expect the platform to enforce KYC and sanctions compliance. Failures may trigger partner audits or relationship termination."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is clear on core rules but leaves several operationally important details ambiguous: daily limit window definition (calendar vs rolling), partially-verified withdrawal rights, sanctions list sources, and what happens to in-flight transactions during account lock. These are not contradictions — they are gaps that need PM clarification before implementation. The core rules (KYC gate, settlement finality, sanctions blocking, account locking) are well-specified and unambiguous.","high_confidence_areas":["KYC verification gate for sending money (BR-1, BR-2)","Settlement finality and irreversibility (BR-5)","Sanctions screening as a hard block (BR-6)","Account locking mechanism and support-only unlock (BR-7)","Notification threshold for transactions over $1,000 (BR-8)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Daily limit window definition (calendar day vs rolling 24 hours)","Whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw","Which sanctions lists are authoritative","Behavior of locked accounts with in-flight transactions","Whether $1,000 notification threshold is inclusive or exclusive (interpreted as exclusive per 'over $1,000')"],"assumptions":["'Over $1,000' means strictly greater than $1,000 (i.e., $1,000.00 does not trigger notification, $1,000.01 does)","Daily limits apply to outbound transfers only, not inbound","The failed login counter tracks consecutive failures and resets on any successful login","Partially-verified accounts CAN send (up to $500/day) but the spec is silent on withdrawal for this tier","T+1 settlement means one business day after initiation","Sanctions screening occurs synchronously at transfer initiation time"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says 'daily transfer limits' but does not define what constitutes a 'day' — calendar day (midnight reset in which timezone?) or rolling 24-hour window. This materially affects limit enforcement behavior.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Engineering","Compliance"],"options":["Calendar day reset at midnight UTC","Calendar day reset at midnight in user's local timezone","Rolling 24-hour window from first transfer"],"recommendation":"Calendar day in UTC is simplest to implement and audit. Recommend UTC midnight reset with clear disclosure to users."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Can partially-verified accounts withdraw funds? The spec explicitly says unverified accounts cannot withdraw, and partially-verified can send up to $500/day, but withdrawal rights for this tier are not stated.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Compliance","Product"],"options":["Partially-verified can withdraw up to $500/day (same as transfer limit)","Partially-verified cannot withdraw (only fully verified can)","Partially-verified can withdraw with a separate, lower limit"],"recommendation":"Partially-verified should NOT be able to withdraw, aligning with the conservative compliance posture implied by the tiered model. Withdrawal should require full KYC."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Which sanctions lists should be used for screening? The spec says 'sanctioned entities' without specifying OFAC SDN, EU consolidated list, UN Security Council list, or others. This determines screening scope and false-positive rates.","affected_stakeholders":["Compliance","Legal","Engineering"],"options":["OFAC SDN list only (US-focused)","OFAC + EU + UN consolidated lists","Delegate to a third-party sanctions screening provider who aggregates all relevant lists"],"recommendation":"Use a third-party screening provider (e.g., ComplyAdvantage, Dow Jones) that aggregates all applicable lists based on operating jurisdictions. This is standard practice."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Notification threshold boundary: does 'over $1,000' include exactly $1,000 or not? Financial systems require precision on boundary conditions.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Engineering"],"options":["Strictly greater than $1,000 (> $1,000)","Greater than or equal to $1,000 (>= $1,000)"],"recommendation":"Interpret as strictly greater than $1,000. If PM intended inclusive, the phrasing would typically be '$1,000 or more'. However, this should be confirmed explicitly."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML/sanctions screening), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.","bounded_context":"Wallet Transfers & Account Security — covers identity verification tiers, transfer execution, sanctions screening, account locking, and
